description: Reconcile two inventories -- e.g. FortiCloud/FortiCare assets vs a CMDB such as ServiceNow -- by a join key (typically a serial number), classify each discrepancy (missing-in-A, missing-in-B, field mismatch, expiring license), write the findings to a reconciliation_results module, and email a CSV report. The archetype fixes the module schema, the connector roles, and the playbook flow; the router/agent fills the deployment-specific slots (module name, join key, recipients, schedule). First pilot instance: FortiCloud assets vs ServiceNow CMDB.
name: reconcile-and-report

parameters.description: Name of the FortiSOAR module that holds reconciliation-result records.
parameters.name: results_module

parameters.description: Human label for source A (e.g. 'FortiCloud assets').
parameters.name: source_a_label

parameters.description: Human label for source B (e.g. 'ServiceNow CMDB').
parameters.name: source_b_label

parameters.description: Field used to match records across the two sources (typically a serial number). Inferred from the module schema when possible, else prompted.
parameters.name: join_key

parameters.description: Email recipient(s) for the CSV report (comma-separated).
parameters.name: recipients

parameters.description: Cron schedule for the out-of-band reconciliation run.
parameters.name: cron

playbook_skeletons.description: Fetch both sources, diff by the join key, write findings to reconciliation_results, and email a CSV report. Abstract trigger (fired by an out-of-band schedule).
playbook_skeletons.name: Reconcile and Report

source.basis: ServiceNow + FortiCare reconciliation pilot (mutable-yawning-fox plan, Form 1)
source.note: Hand-curated (no single reconciliation solution pack exists to harvest). Module field types grounded in real mmd.json (string/text, picklists/picklist, datetime); connector ops research-verified per plan. The diff/CSV step uses the custom 'code-runner' connector (role 'compute') instead of the stock 'code-snippet': code-snippet's sandbox execs at module level (no top-level return) and restricts open() (no CSV file write), which broke this archetype's natural design; code-runner removes both restrictions. code-runner is UNRESTRICTED arbitrary-code execution with filesystem access -- a deliberate trade-off for this trusted, operator-authored archetype. Source: connector-code-runner repo.

when_to_use: Compare two data sources, surface records present in one but not the other plus field mismatches on a join key, persist each finding to a reconciliation-results record, and notify stakeholders with a CSV report. Triggered on a schedule (an out-of-band Celery task pointing at an abstract-trigger playbook), not by an inbound event.
